pokemon:kabutops.name: Kabutops
pokemon:kabutops.summary: Kabutops swam underwater to hunt for its prey in ancient times. The Pokémon was apparently evolving from being a water dweller to living on land as evident from the beginnings of change in its gills and legs.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: Kabutops swam underwater to hunt for its prey in ancient times. The Pokémon was apparently evolving from being a water dweller to living on land as evident from the beginnings of change in its gills and legs.
It is thought that this Pokémon came onto land because its prey adapted to life on land.
It is thought that this Pokémon came onto land because its prey adapted to life on land.
Its sleek shape is perfect for swim­ ming. It slashes prey with its claws and drains the body fluids.
It was able to swim quickly thro­ ugh the water by compactly folding up its razor-sharp sickles.
It is thought that this Pokémon came onto land because its prey adapted to life on land.
KABUTOPS once swam underwater to hunt for prey. It was apparently evolving from being a water dweller to living on land as evident from changes in its gills and legs.
It swims freely through water. It catches prey with its scythe-like arms and drains the victim’s fluids.
In the water, it tucks in its limbs to become more compact, then it wiggles its shell to swim fast.
In the water, it tucks in its limbs to become more compact, then it wiggles its shell to swim fast.
Its sleek shape is perfect for swimming. It slashes prey with its claws and drains their fluids.
A slim and fast swimmer. It sliced its prey with its sharp sickles and drank the body fluids.
A slim and fast swimmer. It sliced its prey with its sharp sickles and drank the body fluids.

Kabutops swam underwater to hunt for its prey in ancient times. The Pokémon was apparently evolving from being a water dweller to living on land as evident from the beginnings of change in its gills and legs.
It is thought that this Pokémon came onto land because its prey adapted to life on land.
It is thought that this Pokémon came onto land because its prey adapted to life on land.
Its sleek shape is perfect for swim­ ming. It slashes prey with its claws and drains the body fluids.

KABUTOPS swam underwater to hunt for its prey in ancient times. The POKéMON was apparently evolving from being a water-dweller to living on land as evident from the beginnings of change in its gills and legs.

KABUTOPS swam underwater to hunt for its prey in ancient times. The POKéMON was apparently evolving from being a water-dweller to living on land as evident from the beginnings of change in its gills and legs.
The cause behind the extinction of this species is unknown. Kabutops were aggressive Pokémon that inhabited warm seas.
With sharp claws, this ferocious, ancient POKéMON rips apart prey and sucks their body fluids.
With sharp claws, this ferocious, ancient Pokémon rips apart prey and sucks their body fluids.
Kabutops slices its prey apart and sucks out the fluids. The discarded body parts become food for other Pokémon.
It swims at speeds of roughly 29 knots, quickly closing in on its prey and slashing into them with its scythes to finish them off.
Its body had begun to change so it could function on land. But it didn’t adapt in time and went extinct.
It is thought that this Pokémon came onto land because its prey adapted to life on land.
It is thought that this Pokémon came onto land because its prey adapted to life on land.
In the water, it tucks in its limbs to become more compact, then it wiggles its shell to swim fast.
A slim and fast swimmer. It slices its prey with its sharp sickles and drinks the body fluids.
A slim and fast swimmer. It slices its prey with its sharp sickles and drinks the body fluids.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: Kabutops swam underwater to hunt for its prey in ancient times. The Pokémon was apparently evolving from being a water dweller to living on land as evident from the beginnings of change in its gills and legs.

pokemon:kabuto.name: Kabuto
pokemon:kabuto.summary: Kabuto is a Pokémon that has been regenerated from a fossil. However, in extremely rare cases, living examples have been discovered. The Pokémon has not changed at all for 300 million years.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: Kabuto is a Pokémon that has been regenerated from a fossil. However, in extremely rare cases, living examples have been discovered. The Pokémon has not changed at all for 300 million years.
It is thought to have inhabited beaches 300 million years ago. It is protected by a stiff shell.
It is thought to have inhabited beaches 300 million years ago. It is protected by a stiff shell.
A POKéMON that was resurrected from a fossil found in what was once the ocean floor eons ago.
Three hundred million years ago, it hid on the sea floor. It also has eyes on its back that glow.
It is thought to have inhabited beaches 300 million years ago. It is protected by a stiff shell.
It is a POKéMON that has been regenerated from a fossil. However, in rare cases, living examples have been discovered. KABUTO have not changed for 300 million years.
This POKéMON was regenerated from the fossil of an ancient creature. It protects itself with a hard shell.
On rare occasions, some have been found as fossils which they became while hiding on the ocean floor.
On rare occasions, some have been found as fossils which they became while hiding on the ocean floor.
A POKéMON that was regenerated from a fossil found in what was once the ocean floor long ago.
A Pokémon that was recovered from a fossil. It used the eyes on its back while hiding on the seafloor.
A Pokémon that was recovered from a fossil. It used the eyes on its back while hiding on the seafloor.
Kabuto is a Pokémon that has been regenerated from a fossil. However, in extremely rare cases, living examples have been discovered. The Pokémon has not changed at all for 300 million years.
It is thought to have inhabited beaches 300 million years ago. It is protected by a stiff shell.
It is thought to have inhabited beaches 300 million years ago. It is protected by a stiff shell.
A POKéMON that was resurrected from a fossil found in what was once the ocean floor eons ago.
KABUTO is a POKéMON that has been regenerated from a fossil. However, in extremely rare cases, living examples have been discovered. The POKéMON has not changed at all for 300 million years.
KABUTO is a POKéMON that has been regenerated from a fossil. However, in extremely rare cases, living examples have been discovered. The POKéMON has not changed at all for 300 million years.
While some say this species has gone extinct, Kabuto sightings are apparently fairly common in some places.
This POKéMON lived in ancient times. On rare occasions, it has been discovered as a living fossil.
This Pokémon lived in ancient times. On rare occasions, it has been discovered as a living fossil.
This species is almost entirely extinct. Kabuto molt every three days, making their shells harder and harder.
This Pokémon became extinct everywhere, except in a few areas. It protects itself with its hard shell.
This Pokémon thrived 300 million years ago. It’s said that living specimens can still be seen in a certain region—a rare sight.
It is thought to have inhabited beaches 300 million years ago. It is protected by a stiff shell.
It is thought to have inhabited beaches 300 million years ago. It is protected by a stiff shell.
It is thought to have inhabited beaches 300 million years ago. It is protected by a stiff shell.
This Pokémon was regenerated from the fossil of an ancient creature. It protects itself with a hard shell.
A POKéMON that was recovered from a fossil. It uses the eyes on its back while hiding on the sea floor.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: Kabuto is a Pokémon that has been regenerated from a fossil. However, in extremely rare cases, living examples have been discovered. The Pokémon has not changed at all for 300 million years.
